reason: Memoization (caching subproblem results) is the defining technique of dynamic programming.
A binary heap is a complete binary tree satisfying the heap-order property; it is not a BST.
Dijkstra is single-source shortest path: from one node to all others. B is a partial/derived use, so D is the fuller description, though D's wording omits 'shortest'.
Figure: NAND(A,B) OR AND(B,C) => X = (A*B)' + (B*C); option A carries the bar over the whole A*B term, option C bars A and B separately.
Truth table gives X = A (00->0, 01->0, 10->1, 11->1); A*(A+B) = A by absorption.
Highlighted cell is column AB=10, row CD=01 => A=1,B=0,C=0,D=1 => A*B'*C'*D.
Ones = m0,m1,m4,m6,m9,m10,m12,m14. m9 only pairs with m1, m10 only pairs with m14, m0 needs its own pair; minimal cover = BD' + B'C'D + ACD' + A'B'C' = 4 groups.
Classic four-NAND network: N=NAND(A,B), X=NAND(NAND(A,N),NAND(N,B)) = A XOR B.
X = NAND(NAND(C,A),NAND(C',B)) = C*A + C'*B, a 2:1 multiplexer (data selector) with C as select.
Time for a gate/inverter to change output state is propagation delay; rise/decay times describe pulse edges.
Ripple-carry adder for n bits: half-adder for LSB, full adders for the other n-1 bits => 1 HA + 15 FA.
A demux routes one input to one of many outputs, and with a counter on the select lines it performs serial-to-parallel conversion; D describes a mux.
Pure ALOHA transmits whenever a frame is ready; slotted ALOHA must wait for the start of a time slot.
ARP maps IP (internet layer) addresses to MAC (link layer) addresses.

Out-of-band control means control info carried on a separate connection from data: that is FTP (port 21 control / port 20 data). TCP carries its control in the segment headers of the same connection (in-band); its URG 'out-of-band data' is explicitly delivered in-band (RFC 6093), so E overreaches.
One record on each side of the relationship is by definition one-to-one.
Activity, use case, collaboration and sequence diagrams are all UML behavioral diagrams.
Collaboration/communication diagrams show object interactions via numbered series of messages; sequence diagrams are not offered as an option here.
Deployment diagrams map software artifacts (files, libraries, tables) onto the hardware topology.
Interaction diagrams (the general family, incl. sequence and collaboration) address the dynamic view of a system; sequence alone is too narrow.
